book_summary: Numbers has chapters 1-4 complete in restored/source-audited draft and mystical companion layers. The opening wilderness block covers the military-age census, camp arrangement around the dwelling-place, Levite substitution for firstborn, clan assignments, redemption accounting, and tabernacle transport duties. Safeguards explicitly reject militarization, census-based human-worth ranking, tribal hierarchy, clerical superiority, age-based exclusion, unsafe sacred labor, and pricing human souls.

chapters.notes: Sinai census, named leaders, military-age roster, total, Levite exemption and sanctuary charge.
Camp standards and marching order with the tent of meeting and Levites at the center.
Aaronic line, Levite census, substitution for firstborn, clan service assignments, redemption silver.
Kohathite, Gershonite, and Merarite transport duties, covering procedures, service-age count, worker-safety and distributed burden.

translation_notes: Numbers/Bemidbar begins in the wilderness with communal organization around Divine presence.
Military-age census language is descriptive of camp organization and must not define human worth.
Those outside the census—women, children, elders, disabled people, and others—remain fully within the covenant community.
The camp is organized around the dwelling-place; Presence, not military power, occupies the center.
Levites are assigned a different service rather than ranked as more valuable.
Levite substitution and redemption silver are ritual accounting, not pricing souls.
Numbers 4 distributes sacred labor by clan and names burdens explicitly.
Covering and sequencing procedures protect workers from dangerous contact; holy service must not become an excuse for unsafe labor.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Numbers 1 census totals require textual-witness and demographic audit.
Military/host terminology requires lexical audit and anti-militarization metadata.
Numbers 2 camp geography and standard arrangement require tabernacle-centered spatial audit.
Numbers 3 Levite-for-firstborn substitution requires Exodus 13 and firstborn theology crosslink.
Numbers 3 redemption silver requires economic and anti-human-valuation audit.
Numbers 4 service-age ranges require labor-history and anti-ageism audit.
Numbers 4 holy-object covering and death warnings require ritual-danger and worker-safety framing.
next_actions: Continue Numbers with chapters 5-6: camp purity, restitution, suspected-adultery ordeal, Nazirite vow, and priestly blessing, with strong anti-misogyny, anti-coercion, and anti-shame safeguards.
